mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is an operator that automatically manages DNS records for Kubernetes services and ingresses by synchronizing them with external DNS providers. It is particularly useful for dynamic environments where services are frequently created and destroyed, ensuring that DNS records are always up-to-date. Key trade-offs include the need for proper permissions on the DNS provider and potential latency in DNS record updates.

steps.title: Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the operator.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

helm repo update

```

> This is the official Bitnami repository for ExternalDNS.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the ExternalDNS operator in the 'kube-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.21.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

```

> Replace 'aws' with your DNS provider as needed.
commands: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.21.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

steps.title: Create an ExternalDNS Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) that defines how ExternalDNS should manage DNS records.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  provider: aws
  zoneType: public
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

```

> Replace 'example.com' with your actual domain.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  provider: aws
  zoneType: public
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ExternalDNS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the ExternalDNS operator and its associated resources. Be aware that DNS records managed by ExternalDNS may not be deleted automatically, depending on your provider's settings.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
  namespace: kube-system
EOF

```

> This command removes the ExternalDNS release.
> This command deletes the ExternalDNS Custom Resource.
commands:
kubectl delete -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
  namespace: kube-system
EOF

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. The upgrade process involves updating the Helm release and verifying the new version is running.

```bash

helm get values external-dns --namespace kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.21.0

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> Backup current values for ExternalDNS.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the new version of ExternalDNS is running.
commands: helm get values external-dns --namespace kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create record: InvalidChangeBatch'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that there is an issue with the DNS record being created, possibly due to conflicting records.

**Fix:**
Check for existing DNS records that may conflict and resolve them.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No credentials found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the ExternalDNS pod does not have the necessary permissions to access the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the IAM role or service account used by ExternalDNS has the correct permissions.

troubleshooting.title: ExternalDNS is not updating DNS records
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the ExternalDNS service account lacks permissions to modify DNS records.

**Fix:**
Check and update the RBAC permissions for the ExternalDNS service account.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates a misconfiguration in the ExternalDNS deployment.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the ExternalDNS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' to identify the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Azure DNS and improved handling of DNS record updates.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pods running in the kube-system namespace with the command 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns' returning a status of 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
